era: 1990-2003. Punk/Garage. E=8 -- fast, loose, humorous, anti-pretentious. Fremont, Seattle. Amphetamine Reptile.

theory_nodes.concept: Amphetamine Reptile Records: first engine appearance at 292. Founded Minneapolis 1986 Tom Hazelmyer. Catalog: Melvins, Helmet, Boss Hog, Unsane. Raw, physical, humorous, anti-pretentious. PNW-Minneapolis axis.

Fremont neighborhood: first engine appearance at 292. Center of the Universe. Troll. Absurdist humor as community aesthetic. Distinct from Capitol Hill: residential, playful, community-oriented vs. urban, serious, arts-focused.
theory_nodes.description: Fremont debuts in engine. Bohemian village, not arts district.

theory_nodes.concept: E=8 streak 290-292: TAAS (post-hardcore), Alcohol Funnycar (punk/grunge), Gas Huffer (punk/garage). Three consecutive E=8 degrees, three distinct characters. E=8 tier of Seattle heavy music documented across three traditions.
theory_nodes.description: E=8 streak completes at 3 consecutive degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: Tom Price: guitarist-vocalist, primary Gas Huffer creative engine. Choppy propulsive rhythmic guitar rooted in blues-punk lineage. Humor as structural element. Anti-pretension as principled aesthetic position.
theory_nodes.description: Tom Price: the Gas Huffer identity.

cross_references.relationship: E=8 streak: second to third degree. Punk/Grunge → Punk/Garage. Different punk traditions at equal energy. Consecutive returns document the punk tier of Seattle's 1990-2003 heavy scene.
E=8 streak anchor. Post-hardcore vs. punk-garage. Capitol Hill vs. Fremont. Genealogical vs. traditional. Three distinct E=8 characters across three degrees.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 + E=7 = E=15. Punk-garage humor and energy paired with tern colony sharp keerr screaming. Laridae arc closes at same degree as E=8 punk streak closes. Both arcs complete at 292.
